pages.full_text:
Westbury Court Garden originally laid out 1696-1711
Restored with original plants H67 NT
(Before Capability Brown) lovely tulips & apples
On home for tea
pages.notes: Left page contains a printed postcard/leaflet for Westbury Court Garden (National Trust) with glued-in image, plus handwritten notes below in blue ink. Postcard describes formal Dutch water garden rediscovered.

pages.full_text:
Friday 29th May
Sandi, Eve, & uncle left at 6 for Coughton Court. We are in a farmer's field. ***
Good foot path walked.
Saturday
We walked about 3 miles by the river left Tinder in the van. Locked in the courtyard block, then explored the house garden - is of the Throckmorton family lived here for 600 years. Catholic. One woman had 19 children who all survived Owen priest's hole. lovely house & garden. Of the 13 people implicated in the gun powder plot 7 were related to the Throckmortons
We supped in the van. It was cold.
Sunday We went to see a nearby NT [?kinetot dovetoe]. (It will Ladder into t) It was amazing. The bird perches were staggered in the way they pointed.
We walked by the river & set home for lunch
pages.notes: Narrative entry spanning Friday through Sunday at Coughton Court. Two words unreadable: one around 'kinetot dovetoe' (possibly 'dovecote'?), one around 'Ladder'. Some words difficult but context suggests entries about garden visits and Throckmorton family history. Yellow decorative element visible at bottom.
